items.title: Stopped Tracking Kong Plugin ZIP Build Artifacts in Gateway 🧹
items.summary: Updated the repository to ignore generated Kong plugin ZIP files and removed the tracked custom-auth bundle from source control. This makes the Lua plugin source the single source of truth and prevents stale packaged artifacts from drifting from current code. The change also documents how to rebuild deployment ZIPs locally before uploading to TSE Kong.

items.title: Consent Install Links Now Use Public Auth Host in Platform Service 🔐
items.summary: Updated AIChat2 auth configuration so consent-card install URLs always use the public auth domain instead of an internal Kubernetes ClusterIP. The change removes the frontend URL fallback to AUTH_BACKEND_URL in worker config and explicitly pins AUTH_FRONTEND_URL in the production deployment manifest. This prevents broken browser consent flows while preserving the existing SSE event format and connector authorization flow.

items.title: Fix blockers for CVM deployment in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves three critical issues preventing the deployment of the `tencent_cvm`. Key changes include correcting environment variable names for Tencent Cloud credentials, addressing a namespace conflict in the `tencentcloud` SDK, and replacing placeholder values with actual resources for the Wisdom Service. As a result, the deployment process is now fully functional for provisioning a Wisdom CVM tenant.

items.title: Reverted custom-auth changes for identity fields in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts recent modifications made to the custom-auth plugin, specifically addressing how identity fields are propagated. The changes included adjustments to the handling of JSON null values, which have now been reverted to the previous implementation. This rollback aims to restore functionality and prevent potential issues with query arguments.

items.title: Align Authorization Handling for Consistency in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the default value of `authorization_id` to an empty string instead of None in the `/auth` path, aligning it with the existing x402 path. This change prevents serialization issues that previously caused crashes in the custom-auth plugin, ensuring smoother operation for non-delegated requests. Additionally, it wraps both `actor_user_id` and `authorization_id` in a fallback to maintain consistent response formatting.

items.title: Patch custom-auth null handling to stop Kong 5xx spikes in Gateway 🚑
items.summary: This update fixes a production regression in the custom-auth plugin where Lua `cjson.null` values for optional identity fields were incorrectly propagated into Kong query params, triggering widespread 5xx errors on POST requests. The patch replaces truthiness checks with strict string type validation for `authorization_id` and `actor_user_id`, preventing userdata values from reaching `set_query()`. The plugin version was bumped from v0.11.0 to v0.11.1, enabling a safe roll-forward after the temporary rollback to v0.10.0.

items.title: Enhance Manage Page with CVM Controls in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces CVM-aware controls on the Manage page, allowing users to start and stop deployments directly. The applicationOperator now supports stop and start deployment actions, enhancing user interaction for Tencent CVM workloads. Additionally, a new MCP Channel card has been added for easy access to API connection details, improving overall usability.

items.title: Enhance Application Configuration Routing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `Dispatcher.vue` component that routes `/applications/:id/config` based on the `service.alias`. Additionally, a new `Wisdom.vue` wizard has been implemented, guiding users through a four-step deployment process while integrating multilingual support for deployment metadata. These changes improve the user experience and streamline the deployment workflow.

items.title: Wisdom SaaS Service Registration Lands with Tencent CVM Metadata in Backend ⚙️
items.summary: Adds an idempotent migration to register the private Wisdom service with Tencent CVM deployment metadata, instance presets, environment token wiring, and exposure settings. Introduces per-preset monthly pricing definitions and a Chinese development guide covering deployment flow and usage. The service remains hidden with placeholder infrastructure values until operators complete configuration.

items.title: Update iOS and Android Versions in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the Nexior package and native versions from 3.35.1 to 3.35.2, ensuring consistency across platforms. The iOS CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION is updated for the upcoming TestFlight upload, and Android version metadata is kept in sync. These changes prepare the app for the next release cycle.

items.title: Enhance PyPI Publishing and CLI Commands in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a streamlined PyPI publishing workflow and new CLI commands for the Wisdom Channel. Key changes include the addition of CI workflows for testing across multiple Python versions, and the implementation of `init` and `doctor` commands for interactive configuration and connectivity checks. These enhancements will improve user experience and deployment efficiency.

items.title: Enhance WAV URL Handling for Better Performance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new helper function to check the availability of upstream WAV URLs, ensuring that playback and download traffic is prioritized from the official source. The changes include modifications to the transferWavFiles and transferAudios functions, which now fall back to a COS mirror only when the upstream URL is unreachable. This approach aims to reduce unnecessary egress costs while maintaining durability.

items.title: Enhance Apple User Profiles with Nicknames and Avatars in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the handling of Apple user profiles by filling in nicknames and generating SVG avatars when necessary. It prioritizes the use of the user's first and last name from Apple, and if unavailable, derives a nickname from the email. This ensures that profiles are visually complete and not left as blank avatars.

items.title: Add Tencent CVM Workload Provider in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Tencent CVM workload provider, allowing for the provisioning of Windows CVM instances via the Tencent Cloud API. Key changes include the addition of new deployment functions, extended deployment results, and updated view actions to support CVM-specific operations. The integration is designed to enhance the deployment capabilities for applications that cannot run in containers, with Wisdom being the first application to utilize this feature.

items.title: Enhance TypeScript Workers with Authorization Fields in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `authorization_id` and `actor_user_id` to the request context and payload for 16 TypeScript-based workers. These additions are crucial for enabling the PlatformGateway to accurately track and enforce application authorization quotas. The changes ensure that services routed through these workers can now properly update their authorization metrics.

items.title: Updated Python Workers to Latest Scaffold Version in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the scaffold version to 2026.5.23.0 across six Python workers, ensuring that the PlatformGateway receives essential fields like `authorization_id` and `actor_user_id` in the `/record` payloads. This change is crucial for enforcing per-grantee quota caps effectively. The workers updated include aichat, asiyun, investing, phoenix, qcloud, and serp.

items.title: Update scaffold version to fix record issues in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This hotfix updates the `acedatacloud-scaffold` version from `0.9.0` to `2026.5.23.0` in both `pyproject.toml` and `requirements.txt`. This change resolves an `AttributeError` that occurred during `record()` calls, ensuring that billing records are properly captured. After deployment, verification steps confirm the correct version is in use and that `record_completed` events are logged correctly.

items.title: Feat(apple): native iOS/Android bridge for Sign in with Apple in Frontend Web ✨
Fix(openai/worker): include authorization_id and actor_user_id in record payload in Platform Service 🐛
Feat: propagate authorization_id and actor_user_id in record payload in Scaffold Python ✨
Fix(custom-auth): propagate authorization_id and actor_user_id upstream in Gateway 🐛
Fix(application): null-safe get_paid against None remaining_amount in Backend 🐛
Feat(suno): prefer upstream WAV URL, COS mirror only as probe-based fallback in Platform Service ✨
Feat(oem): respect externally-written C:\wisdom\.env (cloudbase-init UserData) in Wisdom ✨
Chore(announcement): drop 5-min unread-count polling, fetch once on mount in Frontend Web 🔧
Fix(apple): accept RS256-signed identity_tokens (not ES256) in Backend 🐛
Fix(publisher): isolate Playwright user_data per (user, channel) to stop cross-channel cookie pollution in Publisher 🐛
Plan: Wisdom SaaS — 7-PR roadmap for CVM-based productization in Index 🔧
Feat(auth): accept Bearer header OR ?token= query param in Wisdom ✨
Feat(chat): auto-resume consent card on OAuth return in Nexior ✨
Feat(connections): /connections/install/:id deep-link install page with return_to in Frontend Web ✨
Feat(setting/auth): add Apple to the provider list in Nexior ✨
Feat(apple): add Apple Sign In binding to user profile Third Party in Frontend Web ✨
Feat(apple): add /users/me/update-apple endpoint for account binding in Backend ✨
Refactor(apple): enable by default, hard-code Services ID in Frontend Web 🧹
Refactor(apple): hard-code Services ID default, drop oauth-apple secret in Backend 🛡️
Introduce interactive connector consent card in Nexior ⚡
items.summary: This update adds a new `<ConnectorConsentCard>` that appears when the assistant encounters unsatisfied connector requirements. Users can authorize connectors via OAuth URLs or skip them, with the card reflecting live and resolved states. Key helper functions, tests, and i18n support were added to manage consent entries and build the output contract for worker resumption. Messages and conversation components were updated to integrate the consent flow seamlessly.

items.title: Enhance Apple OAuth Support in Backend 🍏
items.summary: This update introduces the optional `oauth-apple` Kubernetes secret, allowing for multiple Apple OAuth client IDs to be used in both production and test deployments. The `APPLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_IDS` environment variable now supports both the iOS bundle ID and a new web Services ID, facilitating the Sign-in-with-Apple feature for web applications. Existing deployments remain unaffected if the secret is not set, ensuring a smooth transition.

items.title: Enhanced Authorization UI for Superusers in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an additional client-side gate for the Application Authorization UI, restricting access to superusers during the rollout phase. The 'Authorize' button and associated page now require both the feature flag and superuser status, ensuring that regular users cannot access the feature prematurely. This change enhances control over the feature's visibility while maintaining existing backend contracts.

items.title: Enable Application Authorization in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update activates the `FEATURES_ENABLE_APPLICATION_AUTHORIZATION` flag in the production environment, allowing enterprise admins to manage application quotas effectively. With this change, grantees can now log in with their own accounts and utilize shared quotas seamlessly. The implementation is straightforward, requiring no additional code changes or database migrations.

items.title: Enhance Consent Handling and Resume Logic in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the consent request handling in the aichat2 connector by allowing the `tool_results` submit endpoint to resolve pauses from consent requests. Key changes include improved detection of paused states and the clearing of pending consent requests upon submission. New tests have been added to ensure robust functionality and error handling.

items.title: iOS builds now auto-select Xcode 26+ for App Store compliance in Nexior 📱
items.summary: Updated the iOS GitHub Actions workflow to automatically select the latest installed Xcode 26 version before archiving. The new step fails fast when no compatible Xcode version is available, preventing invalid App Store uploads. This ensures iOS builds use the required iOS 26 SDK and avoids App Store Connect 409 validation errors during TestFlight submissions.

items.title: Add `get_connector_status` tool with structured consent pause in Platform Service ⚡️
items.summary: Introduces the `get_connector_status` built-in tool to preflight connector authorizations. Turns now pause with a `consent_request` SSE event when required connectors are unconnected, enabling structured user consent. Fast-path outcomes continue immediately if all requirements are met, while slow-path outcomes trigger the new pause and consent workflow. The change is fully tested with 352 passing tests, including 22 new ones for edge cases and payload validation.

items.title: Disable code signing for all Pods targets in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The Podfile's `post_install` hook was updated to set `CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO` and `CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO` on every Pods target. This prevents CI build failures caused by Capacitor frameworks receiving manual provisioning profiles. Local `pod install` workflows remain unaffected, and the change ensures that only the App target is signed during CI builds. After merging, the next iOS build should successfully archive, export, and upload the IPA to TestFlight.

items.title: Revamp Connector Integration in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the outdated `<mcp-servers>` block with a comprehensive `<connectors>` block that displays the entire connector catalog, including both first-party and third-party MCPs. Each connector is annotated with its connection status, enhancing the model's ability to manage user interactions effectively. Additionally, the update introduces new tests and optimizes the handling of connector statuses for improved performance.

items.title: Add comprehensive Android release runbook in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Introduces a detailed operator guide for Android releases in Nexior, complementing the existing setup documentation. Covers the full CI pipeline, daily release commands, promotion paths from internal to production, versioning rules, and common CI failure troubleshooting. Includes links to validation runs and prior PRs to ensure reproducibility. No code changes were made; this is purely documentation.

items.title: New Internal Connector Catalog Endpoint in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new internal endpoint `GET /internal/v1/connectors/catalog/` that allows authenticated access to all installable, non-hidden connector items. It enhances the aichat2 worker's ability to populate the `<connectors>` block efficiently without user-scoped REST interactions. The implementation includes a lean serializer and comprehensive tests to ensure reliability.

items.title: Version bump to 3.35.1 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update increments the version from 3.35.0 to 3.35.1, ensuring a unique versionCode of 33501 for the upcoming Play Store upload. Key files modified include package.json, build.gradle, and project.pbxproj to reflect the new versioning. The production submission for version 3.35.0 remains unaffected during this process.

items.title: Finalize Android Release Signing Config in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the release signing configuration for the Android app and migrates the deprecated 'track' parameter to 'tracks' in the CI workflow. Key improvements include a new local development setup with a template for signing credentials and enhanced gitignore rules to prevent sensitive information from being committed. Overall, these changes streamline the development process without affecting CI behavior.

items.title: Revamping Connector Consent Flow in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive design plan for the in-chat connector consent UX, enhancing user interactions with a new dialog for authorization. Key decisions include the naming of the tool as `get_connector_status`, leveraging existing catalog data, and ensuring seamless user experience with pause/resume functionality. This plan sets the stage for multiple upcoming PRs across various services, streamlining the integration process.

items.title: Upgrade Java Version for Capacitor 8 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the JAVA_VERSION to 21 in the Android build workflow to support Capacitor 8. The previous JDK 17 setup caused compilation errors due to incompatible source release settings. This change ensures smoother builds and compatibility moving forward.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Translate Functionality in Nexior 🌍
items.summary: This update introduces an auto-translate toggle directly within the Nexior subsite settings, allowing admins to manage translations without switching to the PlatformFrontend dev portal. Key changes include the addition of a new operator and component for translation management, as well as expanded internationalization support across 18 locales. This enhancement streamlines the editing process, ensuring that auto-translated content remains synchronized with user edits.

items.title: Improve card spacing for aspect ratios in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update refines the layout of the aspect ratio selector in the Veo component by adjusting the flex properties. The cards for `16:9` and `9:16` are now displayed side-by-side with a gap, enhancing visual coherence. This change addresses the previously large empty space between the cards, improving the overall user experience.

items.title: Fix serializer field declarations for site data in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of missing derived fields in the site serializers by ensuring that `SerializerMethodField()` attributes are declared directly on each concrete serializer subclass. The fix includes new tests to verify that the fields are correctly included in API responses. Overall, this enhances the functionality of the site data retrieval process.

items.title: Add Sign in with Apple login button in Frontend Web 🍏
items.summary: This update introduces the Sign in with Apple feature as a primary login option on the authentication platform. It aligns with existing login methods like Google and GitHub, ensuring compliance with App Store guidelines for the Nexior iOS App. The implementation includes new components, configuration settings, and internationalization support for multiple languages.

items.title: Add Sign in with Apple support for seamless login in Backend 🍏
items.summary: This update introduces Sign in with Apple to the AuthBackend, addressing a critical requirement for the Nexior iOS app to comply with App Store Review Guideline 4.8. The implementation includes JWT verification of the identity_token and allows users to log in using their Apple accounts, enhancing the authentication options available. Key changes include new model fields, updated settings, and comprehensive tests to ensure functionality.

items.title: Enhance site serializer to include translation sources in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a mixin to the SiteIndexSerializer, allowing it to expose translation source fields alongside the existing detail serializer. As a result, both list and detail endpoints now return consistent data, enabling the auto-translate toggle in Nexior and PlatformFrontend to function correctly. All existing tests pass, and new tests have been added to ensure the mixin's integration is seamless.

items.title: No-op Update for Gemini Docs in M C Ps 🚫
items.summary: This update confirms that the `gemini` service is not mapped in the MCPs, resulting in a no-op. The service mapping was verified, and while the `openapi/gemini.json` exists upstream, there is no corresponding MCP subdirectory. The issue has been resolved by closing it as 'already in sync / not applicable'.

items.title: Enforce Supported Aspect Ratios in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update ensures that the `/veo/videos` workers—`mountsea`, `cqtai`, and `wcnb`—only accept the supported aspect ratios of `16:9` and `9:16`. Previously, unsupported ratios like `1:1` and `3:4` were accepted, leading to errors after credit reservation. Now, clients receive a clear `BadRequest` message at validation time, improving the user experience and reducing unnecessary resource usage.

items.title: Refine aspect ratio for video uploads in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update narrows the aspect ratio options for the `/veo/videos` endpoint to only `16:9` and `9:16`, aligning with Google Veo's upstream limitations. The OpenAPI specification has been modified to reflect this change, ensuring that invalid values are no longer accepted. This will prevent errors related to unsupported aspect ratios during video uploads.

items.title: Streamline Aspect Ratio Options for Veo in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update restricts the aspect ratio selector in Veo to only support `16:9` and `9:16`, aligning with upstream requirements. Previously available options like `1:1`, `4:3`, and `3:4` have been removed to prevent user errors. The default setting remains `16:9`, ensuring a smooth experience for both new and existing users.

items.title: Onboard Kiro Claude as Top-Priority Provider in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Kiro Claude upstream provider at rank 20000, making it the primary backend for six modern Claude models. Key changes include the addition of a new configuration file and updates to the cronjob for API key management. The integration has been validated with successful tests across all models, ensuring robust performance and caching improvements.

items.title: Added iOS App Store Release Manual in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive operator-facing manual for shipping the Nexior iOS app to the App Store, located in the internal planning area. The manual covers essential steps including pre-flight checks, App Store metadata preparation, build and submission processes, and common rejection reasons with mitigations. This documentation aims to streamline the release process for the Nexior app.

items.title: Enhance iOS Compliance by Hiding Non-IAP UI and Adding Privacy Manifest in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update prepares the iOS bundle for App Store submission by hiding non-IAP payment UI elements in compliance with App Store Review Guideline 3.1.1. Additionally, a new PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy file has been added to meet Apple's submission requirements, ensuring user data privacy is properly declared. The changes are gated to only affect the iOS surface, leaving web and Android functionalities intact.

items.title: Enhance Sites Console with Auto-Translate Toggle in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `<AutoTranslateToggle>` component to the General tab of the Sites Console, allowing users to toggle between literal and auto-translated titles and descriptions. It integrates with new backend fields for seamless functionality and ensures graceful degradation in production until backend deployment. The implementation reuses existing internationalization keys, maintaining support for all 11 locales.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Translate Feature in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a preserve-state mode for the auto-translate toggle, ensuring that toggling off retains the previous state during updates. Key changes include enhancements to the `sync_i18n_fields` function and integration of site title and description management. The modifications improve the overall user experience by maintaining translation integrity across various operations.
